prompt: For a technical B2B security-evidence product (kolm: signed, offline-verifiable evidence that clears an AI agent's enterprise security review), should the HERO lead with the MECHANISM (cryptographically signed, offline-verifiable, Ed25519, no server in trust path), the OUTCOME (clear the security review in days, not weeks; close the enterprise deal), or the PAIN (your agent deal is stalled in a 6-8 week security review)? Pull evidence from how the best 2026 security/devtools/compliance companies structure their hero (cite Vanta, Drata, Secureframe, Stytch, WorkOS, Vercel, Tailscale, Sigstore, or similar with URLs). What converts best for the specific buyer here (an AI-agent vendor founder/eng-lead selling into the enterprise)? Give a recommended hero structure: exact eyebrow + headline + subhead + primary CTA, and say what to put above the fold next to it.
content:
**Lead with the OUTCOME (or a tight Pain → Outcome structure), not the raw mechanism.** 

The top 2026 security, compliance, and developer-platform companies (Vanta, Drata, Secureframe, Stytch, WorkOS) overwhelmingly prioritize **outcomes**—especially “enterprise-ready,” faster sales cycles, unblocked deals, trust/proof, and growth acceleration—over technical mechanism details in the hero. Mechanism (how it works, crypto primitives, architecture) appears later in features, “how it works” sections, or sub-bullets. Pain is used lightly as setup, not the dominant headline.[[1]](https://www.vanta.com/)

### Evidence from the cited companies (2026 homepages)
- **Stytch** (closest analog—auth/security for AI agents + enterprise): Hero leads with outcome + audience fit: “The identity platform for humans & AI agents. One integration for authentication, authorization, and security, **making your app enterprise-ready and agent-ready**.” Mechanism (“one integration”) is secondary; the payoff for the exact buyer (devs selling AI/agent tools into enterprise) is front-and-center. CTA: “Watch demo / Talk to an expert.”[[2]](https://stytch.com/)
- **WorkOS** (devtools → enterprise features): “**Your app, Enterprise Ready.**” Headline is pure outcome. Subtext and body emphasize expanding market, shipping enterprise features fast, and unblocking sales. Mechanism (APIs, building blocks, Admin Portal) comes in supporting sections.[[3]](https://workos.com/)
- **Vanta** (compliance/evidence automation): Hero opens with frameworks + “**Trust is everything. Earn and prove it with Vanta.**” Strong outcome language around getting audit-ready fast, unblocking sales cycles, and AI/agentic automation. Testimonials highlight sales enablement.[[1]](https://www.vanta.com/)
- **Drata** (agentic trust/compliance): “**The Agentic Trust Management Platform.**” Hero and stats emphasize outcomes: hours saved, revenue accelerated, faster security reviews, and continuous proof. Body reinforces “accelerate sales cycles.”[[4]](https://drata.com/)
- **Secureframe**: “**Build trust. Unlock growth.**” Headline + subhead focus on outcomes (automate compliance, accelerate sales cycles, reduce risk). Automation is the “how,” not the lead.[[5]](https://secureframe.com/)

These patterns convert best for the exact buyer persona (AI-agent vendor founder/eng lead selling into enterprise) because that person’s #1 job is **closing deals that are stuck in security reviews**. They already understand the pain; they need the credible promise that this solves it faster/better than alternatives (or manual evidence). Leading with crypto primitives (Ed25519, offline verification, no server in trust path) risks sounding like a feature to a buyer who is not yet a security engineer—they want the deal velocity first. Pure pain (“your deal is stalled”) can feel negative or assumptive on a landing page.

### Recommended hero structure for kolm
**Eyebrow** (small, above headline): For AI Agent Vendors

**Headline** (bold, large): Close enterprise deals in days, not weeks.

**Subhead**: Signed, offline-verifiable evidence that clears security reviews instantly—no questionnaires, no back-and-forth.

**Primary CTA** (prominent button): Get started (or “See a sample” / “Request demo”)

**What to put above the fold next to it** (right column or integrated visual):
- A clean visual mockup or animation of the signed evidence artifact (e.g., a compact “kolm evidence” badge or QR/code snippet) + one-line verification UI (“Verified offline • Ed25519 • No server required”).
- Or a short trust bar / logos of enterprises or agent platforms already using similar patterns.
- Keep the visual minimal and technical-but-approachable—reinforce credibility without overwhelming with code.

This structure mirrors the winning pattern (outcome-first, buyer-specific language, light mechanism in support) while directly addressing the stalled-deal reality for AI-agent sellers. Mechanism details belong in a “How it works” section or expandable card immediately below the fold.
